column1: # Abbreviation / acronym expansion table for normalize_transcripts.py  (V3 output)
#   body  = apply to spoken text lines only  (default; never touches spk_NNNN label lines)
#   all   = apply everywhere (not useful here since speaker lines are now spk IDs)
#   short_form ending with '.'  → matched as  \b<base>\. followed by whitespace/punctuation
#   short_form without '.'      → matched as whole word  \b<form>\b  (case-insensitive)
# Entries are applied longest-first to avoid partial matches.
# Add, remove, or edit lines freely.  Lines starting with '#' are ignored.

column4: Indian judicial honorific (straight-apostrophe form); e.g. "Hon'ble Court"
Indian judicial honorific (apostrophe dropped by PDF extractor → space)
Case citation; e.g. "Union of India vs. State"

column1: # No.  -- MOVED TO CODE: expands to "number" only when followed by a digit (rule 5)
# Art. -- MOVED TO CODE: expands to "article" only when followed by a digit (rule 4)

column4: Part of Constitution; e.g. "Pt. III" → "part III"

column1: # para -- MOVED TO CODE: expands to "paragraph" only when NOT followed by a digit (rule 1)

column4: Speaker labels are now spk IDs so body-only is sufficient

column1: # SLP -- DISABLED per audio review (rule 2): spoken as letters, not expanded

column4: VERIFY — IA also appears as initials (e.g. a person named I.A.)
VERIFY — may conflict with initials (e.g. A.G. Noorani)
